steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The logical relationship is cause-effect (evidence to conclusion). Sentence 1 presents the evidence: Transantarctic glaciers are at least 60 million years old. The blank needs a transition signaling that the researchers drew a conclusion from that evidence.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "By contrast": signals opposition. But sentence 2 is not in conflict with sentence 1 — it is the logical conclusion that follows from it. ✗
  • Choice B — "Thus": signals that sentence 2 is the conclusion drawn from the evidence in sentence 1 (glaciers predate the 34-million-year ice sheet), so fits. ✓
  • Choice C — "Even so": signals concession — that sentence 2 is true despite sentence 1. Sentence 2 is not a concession; it is a direct inference. ✗
  • Choice D — "Nevertheless": signals that sentence 2 holds true despite some obstacle in sentence 1. No obstacle exists; sentence 1 is the supporting evidence. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
